name: Jay Inslee
subject: We have a choice to make.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
Are we going to rise to the occasion and save our world from the climate crisis?

| [](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7a502/2f5ea88d/796e2fc3/2cbdf1b6/1966750938/VEsH/ "https://facebook.com/jayinslee/photos/a.447222018465/10157024794423466/") Friend, We are the first generation to feel the sting of climate change, and we are the last generation that can do something about it. As a nation -- as a people -- we have a choice to make: Are we going to rise to the occasion and save our country and world from the climate crisis? Or are we going to shrink from this challenge -- and face dire consequences? I know my answer. I know that our nation needs leaders who will put fighting climate change at the top of the agenda. [](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7a502/2f5ea88d/796e2fc3/2cbdf1b6/1966750938/VEsE/ "https://facebook.com/jayinslee/photos/a.447222018465/10157024794423466/") [**Share what I had to say in the _Washington Post_ about why we must make climate change our top priority, and sign on to be part of the movement.**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7a502/2f5ea88d/796e2fc3/2cbdf1b6/1966750938/VEsF/ "https://facebook.com/jayinslee/photos/a.447222018465/10157024794423466/") Friend, we must reclaim our future by leading the work to combat climate change. I know this will be a defining challenge for us. It's why I take this responsibility to act so seriously. Working together, we can make tremendous progress. I've seen firsthand what's possible when you invest in clean energy. I've seen how people can come together to move towards 100 percent clean power. But it's not enough. We need leaders who will launch these efforts on an enormous scale. We need leaders who will summon the full energies of our nation to realize what the science is demanding of us. And we need a Democratic Party that will support this ambitious and crucial agenda. [**Share my _Washington Post_ op-ed and join the discussion about why we need leaders who will treat the climate emergency with the weight it requires. **](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7a502/2f5ea88d/796e2fc3/2cbdf1b6/1966750938/VEsC/ "https://facebook.com/jayinslee/photos/a.447222018465/10157024794423466/") Let's make the right choice on this one -- so we don't have to see what happens if we don't. Very truly yours, Jay Inslee | [SHARE THE OP-ED »](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7a502/2f5ea88d/796e2fc3/2cbdf1b6/1966750938/VEsD/ "https://facebook.com/jayinslee/photos/a.447222018465/10157024794423466/")

name: Mary Mancini
subject: Marsha Blackburn is ignoring calls from furloughed federal workers
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE TENNESSEE DEMOCRATIC PARTY, DR
body:
Peter,

Amid the longest government shutdown in U.S history, Tennessee Republican Senator Marsha Blackburn still does not have a not have a Nashville office, Senate email or Senate online contact form,**meaning Tennessee federal workers and affected by the shutdown STILL have not been able to reach Senator Blackburn.**

[](https://act.myngp.com/el/l_cPD5VOq5Iia70Hr2BadJPPaG8bUApYBPKlZTkKRb8=/R-FuNuolsAUwL6J3eJStWUSSi2Y_cAfA1L8XIG97E8s=?midqs=xXP7evizoGOltvj4Py4JPw==)

Yesterday, we learned that nearly a month into her term, Blackburn:
* Does not have an office email
* Does not have a Nashville office
* Hasn't returned any phone calls or hand written letters from furloughed federal employees.

**7,000 Tennessean federal workers are either furloughed or going without pay, and Marsha Blackburn can 't even be bothered to return their calls.**

| | | Team - It's unconscionable that Coast Guard members, here in North Carolina and across the country, were not paid because of President Trump's shutdown.

Roy is standing up to demand an end to this manufactured crisis so we can take care of the men and women who keep us safe. _**[Will you stand with Roy and add your name today to call on President Trump to end this shutdown?](http://act.roycooper. Begin Forward from Roy Cooper:
* * *
Team,

The federal government has been closed 33 days, marking the longest government shutdown in history. That means 800,000 federal workers are now missing their paycheck, and North Carolinians across the state are feeling the impact.

When I visited the Coast Guard base at Elizabeth City last year, I was humbled by the courageous work our Coast Guard members do to keep us safe every day.

It is unthinkable that patriotic Americans who have sacrificed so much to serve our country could be put in a situation where they can't pay their bills and provide for their families. I won't stand for it.

Last week, I sent a letter to President Trump urging him to end this government shutdown so we can stop the suffering and take care of the service members and public servants who are impacted by this shutdown. **Now, I 'm reaching out to you because we must stand together.**

Roy

name: =?utf-8?Q?Indiana=20Republican=20Party?=
subject: 2 Years
body:
Two years. This week marks the two-year anniversary of Donald J. Trump standing on the steps of the U.S. Capitol, taking the Oath of Office... | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/indiana/2-years?e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| |
---
| [ ](http://)
---
| **_2 Years_** Two years. This week marks the two-year anniversary of Donald J. Trump standing on the steps of the U.S. Capitol, taking the Oath of Office, and becoming our nation’s 45th president of the United States.

Since then, America has experienced the roaring economic growth we’ve been enjoying in Indiana for years. Here’s just a few of the top accomplishments President Trump is delivering for Americans:
* More than 4.6 million jobs have been created since President Trump’s election.
* Unemployment rate remains at 3.9 percent, which is nearly the lowest rate in almost 50 years.
* Under President Trump, job openings outnumber the unemployed for the first time on record.
* Americans have more healthcare freedom thanks to the President signing legislation that ended Obamacare’s individual mandate penalty.
* President Trump exceeded his promise to roll back two regulations for every new one created, eliminating 12 regulations for every new one in 2018 & 22 regulations for every new one in 2017.
* President Trump has appointed two Supreme Court justices, Justice Neil Gorsuch and Justice Brett Kavanaugh.

Under President Trump’s leadership, there’s no question that we’re seeing a strong record of promises made and promises kept. It’s been two years of making America great! Check out this longer [list of President Trump’s record of achievements](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=f63cef434b&e=1acd9bb3d6) for the American people now!

-Kyle Hupfer
---
|
---
| | | **GOVERNOR HOLCOMB'S WEEK THAT WAS**
---
|
---
|
---
---
| **_Indiana: A Global Tech Leader!_** Indiana is a global leader for high-wage technology jobs! Last year, 62 tech firms committed to creating more than 5,700 new Hoosier jobs – and even more commitments came this week. Under **Governor Eric Holcomb’s** leadership, Indiana is continuing to build the perfect environment for growth. Just this week:

_**More New Jobs On Their Way**_
**Governor Holcomb** was on hand as the [software firm Kernauno announced](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=ebabf55306&e=1acd9bb3d6) that it was expanding here in Indiana, picking the state as its official headquarters. Kernauno plans to expand its staff to a total of 140 Hoosiers in the coming years, with wages above local and state averages! The company said that expanding in Indiana made sense, citing the incredible talent pool here and positive business environment supported by Indiana’s state government **_Computer Science Educaton on Showcase_**
**Governor Holcomb** also welcomed middle school and high school computer science students to the Statehouse this week for the Indiana K-12 Computer Science Showcase. Students from across the state came together to showcase their web, app and computing device projects – and there was some pretty impressive stuff. With these young talented Hoosiers, there’s no question that the next big tech innovation will be developed here in Indiana!
---
|
---
| [ ](http://)
---
| **_Senator Mike Braun Presides Over the U.S. Senate_** Yesterday, **Indiana’s new U.S. Senator** ,**Mike Braun** , was front-and-center for a big job – presiding over the U.S. Senate for a vote on a legislative package proposed by **President Donald Trump** to secure the border and fully reopen the federal government.
---
|
---
| [ ](http://)
---
| **_Indiana Delegation Receives Committee Assignments_** Your Hoosier members of the 116th U.S. Congress were sworn in this month and have received their committee assignments! The Indiana Delegation will be working hard to deliver results for all Americans with the following committee assignments:
* **Senator Todd Young** will continue to serve on the U.S. Senate Committees on Foreign Relations; Commerce, Science, and Transportation; and Small Business and Entrepreneurship. He has also been added to the U.S. Senate Committee on Finance this session.
* **Senator Mike Braun** has been assigned to serve on the Agriculture, Nutrition & Forestry Committee; Committee on the Budget; Committee on Health, Education, Labor & Pensions; Environment & Public Works Committee and the Special Committee on Aging.
* **Congresswoman Jackie Walorski** will continue to serve on the House Ways and Means Committee.
* **Congressman Jim Banks** will serve on the House Armed Services Committee; the House Committee on Education and the Workforce; and the House Veterans Affairs Committee in the 116th Congress.
* **Congressman Jim Baird** has been selected to serve on the House Agriculture Committee, and the Science, Space, and Technology Committee.
* **Congresswoman Susan Brooks** will continue to serve on the Energy and Commerce Committee.
* **Congressman Greg Pence** has been assigned to the Transportation and Infrastructure Committee, and the House Committee on Foreign Affairs.
* **Congressman Larry Bucshon** will continue to serve on the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.
* **Congressman Trey Hollingsworth** will continue to serve on the House Financial Services Committee.

clean_body: Two years. This week marks the two-year anniversary of Donald J. Trump standing on the steps of the U.S. Capitol, taking the Oath of Office... | | | | | | http:// | 2 Years Two years. This week marks the two-year anniversary of Donald J. Trump standing on the steps of the U.S. Capitol, taking the Oath of Office, and becoming our nation’s 45th president of the United States. Since then, America has experienced the roaring economic growth we’ve been enjoying in Indiana for years. Here’s just a few of the top accomplishments President Trump is delivering for Americans: More than 4.6 million jobs have been created since President Trump’s election. Unemployment rate remains at 3.9 percent, which is nearly the lowest rate in almost 50 years. Under President Trump, job openings outnumber the unemployed for the first time on record. Americans have more healthcare freedom thanks to the President signing legislation that ended Obamacare’s individual mandate penalty. President Trump exceeded his promise to roll back two regulations for every new one created, eliminating 12 regulations for every new one in 2018 & 22 regulations for every new one in 2017. President Trump has appointed two Supreme Court justices, Justice Neil Gorsuch and Justice Brett Kavanaugh. Under President Trump’s leadership, there’s no question that we’re seeing a strong record of promises made and promises kept. It’s been two years of making America great! Check out this longer for the American people now! -Kyle Hupfer | | | | GOVERNOR HOLCOMB'S WEEK THAT WAS | | | Indiana: A Global Tech Leader! Indiana is a global leader for high-wage technology jobs! Last year, 62 tech firms committed to creating more than 5,700 new Hoosier jobs – and even more commitments came this week. Under Governor Eric Holcomb’s leadership, Indiana is continuing to build the perfect environment for growth. Just this week: More New Jobs On Their Way Governor Holcomb was on hand as the that it was expanding here in Indiana, picking the state as its official headquarters. Kernauno plans to expand its staff to a total of 140 Hoosiers in the coming years, with wages above local and state averages! The company said that expanding in Indiana made sense, citing the incredible talent pool here and positive business environment supported by Indiana’s state government Computer Science Educaton on Showcase Governor Holcomb also welcomed middle school and high school computer science students to the Statehouse this week for the Indiana K-12 Computer Science Showcase. Students from across the state came together to showcase their web, app and computing device projects – and there was some pretty impressive stuff. With these young talented Hoosiers, there’s no question that the next big tech innovation will be developed here in Indiana! | | http:// | Senator Mike Braun Presides Over the U.S. Senate Yesterday, Indiana’s new U.S. Senator ,Mike Braun , was front-and-center for a big job – presiding over the U.S. Senate for a vote on a legislative package proposed by President Donald Trump to secure the border and fully reopen the federal government. | | http:// | Indiana Delegation Receives Committee Assignments Your Hoosier members of the 116th U.S. Congress were sworn in this month and have received their committee assignments! The Indiana Delegation will be working hard to deliver results for all Americans with the following committee assignments: Senator Todd Young will continue to serve on the U.S. Senate Committees on Foreign Relations; Commerce, Science, and Transportation; and Small Business and Entrepreneurship. He has also been added to the U.S. Senate Committee on Finance this session. Senator Mike Braun has been assigned to serve on the Agriculture, Nutrition & Forestry Committee; Committee on the Budget; Committee on Health, Education, Labor & Pensions; Environment & Public Works Committee and the Special Committee on Aging. Congresswoman Jackie Walorski will continue to serve on the House Ways and Means Committee. Congressman Jim Banks will serve on the House Armed Services Committee; the House Committee on Education and the Workforce; and the House Veterans Affairs Committee in the 116th Congress. Congressman Jim Baird has been selected to serve on the House Agriculture Committee, and the Science, Space, and Technology Committee. Congresswoman Susan Brooks will continue to serve on the Energy and Commerce Committee. Congressman Greg Pence has been assigned to the Transportation and Infrastructure Committee, and the House Committee on Foreign Affairs. Congressman Larry Bucshon will continue to serve on the House Committee on Energy and Commerce. Congressman Trey Hollingsworth will continue to serve on the House Financial Services Committee. | | | Lugar Series Next Generation Do you know a young woman between the ages of 13-18 interested in learning more about government and politics? New year. New speaker of the House. New Nancy Pelosi phone background. Get it now >> We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118ca2c4/24c69fd9/2f64f625a/18b2e006/3543449406/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WRENE150N_digital_email_20190125_SMSPelosi_inact&source=WRENE150N_digital_email_20190125_SMSPelosi_inact")

Peter,

New year. New speaker of the House. New phone background.

Now that our women have single-handedly flipped the House from red to blue and given Nancy Pelosi back the gavel, our phones are in need of a refresh.

**Help celebrate her leadership. Text SPEAKER to 47717 to get our new phone background featuring Speaker Pelosi -- and **_that_** coat and **_those_** sunglasses.**
[](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118ca2c4/24c69fd9/2f64f625a/18b2e007/3543449406/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/s/20190125_email_SMSPelosi?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2&utm_campaign=WRENE150N_digital_email_20190125_SMSPelosi_inact&source=WRENE150N_digital_email_20190125_SMSPelosi_inact")
Away from your phone? **[Sign up here and we'll text you your new background.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118ca2c4/24c69fd9/2f64f625a/18b2e000/3543449406/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/s/20190125_email_SMSPelosi?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+Sign+up+here+and+well+text+you+your+new+&utm_campaign=WRENE150N_digital_email_20190125_SMSPelosi_inact&source=WRENE150N_digital_email_20190125_SMSPelosi_inact")**

Thanks,

Olivia Noble
Digital Engagement Director, EMILY's List

Receive periodic messages from EMILY's List. Text STOP to cancel & HELP for help. Message & data rates may apply.
